records.lobbyist_name: National Infertility Support and Information Group
records.specific_details: NISIG emailed members of the Oireachtas our 2026 pre-budget submission. Key asks in the pre-budget submission included funding our NISIG Helpline, a request for a wider and more sufficiently funded public funded IVF scheme. Also, that eligibility and criteria be broad, equitable, inclusive and reflective of those who seek infertility treatment. Also, that donor conception must be ready to roll out in conjunction with the establishment of the new Regulatory Authority
records.intended_results: To secure future funding for the NISIG Helpline, to maintain this crucial support for those in need of assistance particularly at times of great emotional stress.::NISIG are calling for a wider and more sufficiently funded public funding system for IVF including significantly increased funding for the fertility hubs nationwide::NISIG asks that eligibility and criteria for public funding for IVF are broad, equitable, inclusive and reflective of those who seek infertility treatment.  This includes a review of the current criteria based on data from the last 2 years, along with areas such as funding for patients requiring PGT-M and PGT-SR, and additional cycles of IVF for couples with no children. Donor conception must be ready to roll out in conjunction with the establishment of the new Regulatory Authority. ::There is no provision for specific support by the State health services for those going through fertility treatment and NISIG receives no Government funding for the vital support s

records.specific_details: Uplift members emailed members of the Committee on Foreign Affairs and Trade and asked them to perform full pre-legislative scrutiny of the changes to the Arbitration Act that would allow the Government to ratify the CETA trade deal. Uplift members also emailed members of the cabinet asking them asking them not to give cabinet approval to amend the Arbitration Act.
records.intended_results: That the Committee on Foreign Affairs and Trade would not waive crucial pre-legislative scrutiny on changes to the Arbitration act, making it harder for the Government to ratify the CETA trade deal. That cabinet approval would be withheld for amending the Arbitration Act.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science
Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science

lobbying_activities: Minister Lawless officially launched the Business of Horsepower: Staffing the Equine Sector report for Horse Sport Ireland at our offices. On the same day, HSI also held a meeting with the Minister to present the key findings of the report, highlight the existing skills gaps within the sector, and ways to improve training and standards. HSI's Skillnet application was also discussed.Opportunities and challenges for the sport horse sector were also raised. Gave update on horse passporting. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Youth work funding and Budget 2026
records.intended_results: Meetings to discuss NYCI Priorities for Budget 2026 & inclusion in party policy documents, as well as in Budget 2026 announcements by Department of Education and Youth::Ensure sufficient capital investment in youth work via the National Development Plan::Information on youth work funding broken down by county::Ensure budget issues facing young people are addressed in Budget 2026
